summary: This notebook implements a spatio-temporal Transformer model for NFL player trajectory prediction, centered around a deterministic geometric baseline that calculates expected player endpoints using physics and role-specific rules. Instead of predicting raw coordinates, the model learns residual corrections to this geometric prior, significantly reducing the optimization landscape. It combines 167 hand-crafted features—including route clustering, weighted neighbor embeddings, and temporal statistics—processed through sliding windows and per-fold scaling. The pipeline uses a 10-fold GroupKFold validation scheme, trains with a time-decayed Huber loss, and averages predictions across folds for final submission. Readers should learn how to integrate domain-derived geometric priors with deep learning to stabilize trajectory forecasting and reduce model complexity.
competition_flow: Loads NFL play CSVs, computes 167 spatio-temporal and geometric features, trains a custom STTransformer across 10 GroupKFold splits using a time-decayed Huber loss, and averages fold predictions for final trajectory submission.
data_reading: Reads `train/input_2023_w*.csv` and `train/output_2023_w*.csv` from a Kaggle input directory using `pd.read_csv` and `pd.concat`, with an optional debug mode that filters to a subset of plays.
data_processing: Computes velocity/acceleration from speed/direction, clips coordinates to field boundaries, handles missing values via forward fill/mean padding, scales features per fold using `StandardScaler`, creates sliding windows of 10 frames with padding, and computes temporal lags/rolling statistics.
features_engineering: 154 base features (physical stats like BMI/momentum, opponent distances, mirror offsets, route clustering labels via KMeans, GNN-lite weighted neighbor embeddings, temporal lags/rolling stats/EMA) + 13 geometric features (endpoint coordinates, required velocity/acceleration, velocity error, alignment metrics).
models: STTransformer (custom PyTorch implementation with TransformerEncoder, positional encoding, attention pooling, and a ResidualMLPHead with residual blocks)
loss_functions: TemporalHuber (Huber loss with exponential time decay weighting)
ensembling: Averages the trajectory predictions across all 10 fold models before clipping coordinates to field boundaries.
insights: Training the model to predict corrections to a deterministic geometric baseline is more effective than predicting raw coordinates.
Role-specific geometric rules (e.g., receivers targeting ball landing, defenders mirroring receivers) provide strong priors that reduce the learning burden.
Weighted neighbor embeddings and route clustering capture spatial and behavioral patterns without full GNN computation.
